samples.xml_description:
Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output for the product. shifts[3:0] Counter for shifts. Addition result. The module performs multiplication of A and B using the shift-and-add method.
  • Initialization: On reset, the shifts counter is set to 0, and the add register is set to 0.
  • Processing: The algorithm iterates through each bit of the multiplier (B). For each bit that is set (1), the multiplicand (A) is added to the product at the corresponding shifted position.
  • Shifting: After each addition, the multiplicand is logically shifted left by one bit to prepare for the next addition, simulating the traditional multiplication process.
  • Output: The final product is stored in the output port, which is 16 bits wide to accommodate the maximum possible product of two 8-bit numbers.

   17 |                 partial_product = partial_product + (A << shift_count);
      |                                                        ^~
